title: NOD-MEG
description: The Natural Object Dataset (NOD) integrates magnetoencephalography (MEG) and electroencephalography (EEG) data collected from human subjects viewing naturalistic object images from ImageNet. This multimodal neuroimaging resource complements previously collected fMRI data from the same subjects and stimuli, enabling investigation of neural mechanisms underlying object recognition with both high spatial and temporal resolution. The dataset encompasses diverse naturalistic stimuli and a broad subject pool, facilitating comparative analyses of neural activation patterns across stimuli and individuals.
methods_description: MEG and EEG data were collected from subjects passively viewing naturalistic object images presented from the ImageNet database. Raw data are stored in BIDS format with multiple sessions and runs per subject. Preprocessing was performed using MNE software (version 1.7.1), with preprocessed time series and epoched data available in the derivatives directory.

funding_references.funder_name: National Natural Science Foundation of China
